name: The White House
subject: Tax Reform Means Better Business for America
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c21ad18555fe8e320607a7127adc62cfb64630afc1cedd19cc908df4212a8d1b9b7bb2af8675fe04422456ae0ef9bf773027f08c8b7f9222

President Trump’s tax reform plan will jumpstart America’s economic engine by making it the most desirable country in the world for businesses to invest and grow. By lowering the corporate tax rate from 35 to 20 percent, the “American Model” will encourage businesses to return from overseas, bringing trillions of dollars into the American economy. To learn more about the Unified Framework for Tax Reform, visit
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c21ad18555fe8e32c931514ef6c178884d98bb9f950cd065222599f2c92f02f02b8dfd8b44ed30c105734ee7688736c1f78aabb8bb0199df
whitehouse.gov/taxreform.

---------------------

Captain Gary M. Rose Receives Medal of Honor

On Monday, President Donald J. Trump awarded the Congressional Medal of Honor to Captain Gary Michael “Mike” Rose (retired). Rose, a United States Army Captain, received the highest military honor for conspicuous gallantry during the Vietnam War, where he risked his life to administer lifesaving aid to his comrades in spite of severe injury and enemy fire. From September 11 through September 14, 1970, while his unit was engaged with a much larger force deep in enemy-controlled territory, then-Sergeant Rose repeatedly ran into the line of enemy fire to provide critical medical aid to his comrades, using his own body on one occasion to shield a wounded American from harm.

On the final day of the mission, although wounded himself, Sergeant Rose voluntarily exposed himself to enemy fire while moving wounded personnel to the extraction point, loading them into helicopters, and helping to repel an enemy assault on the American position. As he boarded the final extraction helicopter, intense enemy fire hit the helicopter, causing it to crash shortly after takeoff. Again, ignoring his own injuries, Sergeant Rose pulled the helicopter crew and members of his unit from the burning wreckage and provided medical aid until another extraction helicopter arrived.

“[Y]our will to endure, your love for your fellow soldier, your devotion to your country inspires us all,” the President said. “Nations are formed out of the strength and patriotism that lives in the hearts of our heroes.”

Read more
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c21ad18555fe8e323e93ff8ead68a7e9bd287a8af37f68bf4501aa35e1cade3e7183efc2607a166602106d6919acdc77008add91ccdae7e0
here.

Vice President Participates in Commemoration of 1983 Tragedy
Vice President Mike Pence delivered remarks at the Marine Barracks in Washington, D.C., on Monday morning to commemorate the 34th anniversary of the 1983 bombing of the Marine barracks in Beirut, Lebanon. Vice President Pence praised the courage of the Marines who were sent to Lebanon to help protect the innocent and prevent a civil war from becoming an even greater tragedy. “[B]ecause of the principles for which they stood and the peace for which they strived, these heroes aroused the attention of great evil,” Pence said, describing what was the beginning of the global war on terror.

The Vice President paid his respects to the fallen and commended those who are currently fighting to defend American freedom.

Creating Jobs, Creating Growth, and Offering Relief to Families
On Monday, Advisor Ivanka Trump attended a tax reform town hall in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, where she discussed the Trump Administration’s unified tax reform plan. She was joined by U.S. Treasurer Jovita Carranza as she delivered remarks to a crowd of roughly 250 attendees.

“There are so many elements squarely targeted at creating jobs, creating growth, and offering relief to our middle-income families,” Trump said, as she continued to tout the merits of the tax-overhaul effort.

The tax reform plan is aimed at simplifying the tax code and increasing U.S. competitiveness on the global stage. Tax reform will provide middle-class relief by permanently reducing tax rates and it will lower the corporate tax rate and give American businesses the competitive edge in the global market. The tax code will be simple, fair, and easy, eliminating loopholes and ensuring that 90% of Americans will be able to file their taxes on one sheet of paper. Finally, the tax reform plan will bring back trillions of dollars in wealth parked overseas, allowing those dollars to be re-invested in American workers, jobs, and companies.

President Trump’s tax reform plan is pro-growth, pro-jobs, pro-worker, and pro-American. Learn more about his tax reform plan
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c21ad18555fe8e32e045506e62e86e2a8014f39c57b408b4b0ab1e1027d2cd5ad0292e645058ef96a59dbb22f0d8fcd5f122a670651b63af
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump presents the Medal of Honor to Retired U.S. Army Captain Gary M. Rose | October 23, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Today, winners of the National Minority Enterprise Development Week Awards Program will be visiting the White House for ceremonial recognition by President Trump and Secretary of Commerce Wilbur Ross. These awards celebrate the achievements of minority entrepreneurs and recognize individuals and organizations that are committed to advancing global minority business enterprise.

Later, President Trump will also participate in the swearing-in ceremony of Callista Gingrich as U.S. Ambassador to the Holy See. And this afternoon, the President will head to the Capitol and participate in the Senate Republican Policy Lunch to discuss tax reform.

**Pitch in $3 or more to help elect Democrats in every state.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523a5c/4067b0a/4547d5af4/7e6403cd/3985901548/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171024_joc_nd2&source=em_20171024_joc_nd2&refcode=em_20171024_joc_nd2")**

America deserves leadership that knows certain things, Derek.

Like a Congress that knows affordable health care is a right, not a privilege -- and that women should be able to make their own health care decisions. Or a president who understands that protecting the environment and listening to scientists is crucial for future generations. We need leadership that knows that tax cuts for the wealthiest Americans don't actually trickle down to help the rest of the country at all, no matter how much Republicans insist it'll happen.

Instead, we have Donald Trump and his Republican cronies who are dead set on doing only what's right for them: Sabotaging our health care system, refusing to take steps to protect our planet, and pushing for tax cuts for millionaires, billionaires, and corporations.

That's why we need you, Derek. Pitch in $3 right now and commit to electing Democrats who will fight for what's right for all Americans.

name: Tim Kaine
subject: A reasonable and comprehensive approach
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek,

As we grapple with the grief brought on by yet another mass shooting to become the worst in modern American history, people are searching for concrete steps we can take to prevent these massacres in the future. We're hearing a lot of speeches, but seeing almost nothing done to protect more Americans from becoming victims in future tragedies.

The time for empty words is over -- it's time to take action. We need to get started on passing commonsense gun safety legislation. The very least we can do is pass a ban on bump stocks, which allow semi-automatic weapons to perform like fully-automatic weapons.

**It's time for the reasonable majority to step up and be heard. If we don't, it will only be a matter of time before we're awakened again by the next gun tragedy to hit the morning news.**

**[Will you add your name to mine and sign this petition calling for reasonable gun safety legislation?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=1DC0o5giy1Uo%2BMualk7hH7dIWvw3tO%2B5)**

A Virginian, James Madison, drafted the Bill of Rights, and we in the Commonwealth understand the Second Amendment. But just as we know that the First Amendment's protection of free speech gives no blanket protection to slander or hate speech, we also understand that the individual right to bear arms is tempered by a responsibility to protect our society.

We must be open to not only expanding mental health services, but strengthening background checks, and placing limits on combat-style weapons and high-capacity magazines. This is about keeping Americans safe. And it's time Congress stepped up to protect our people. That's why I've signed on to cosponsor a bill called the Automatic Gunfire Prevention Act, which will close the bump stock loophole that currently allows semi-automatic weapons to fire at a rate of automatic weapons -- which is up to 700 rounds per minute. The reality is that no civilian needs access to that level of firepower. It's just common sense.

**[If you agree that it's time to take action to keep our neighborhoods safe, will you sign our petition calling for Congress to take up gun safety legislation?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=e5AVR5ykMSwGBkOTSoc3j%2FmfYD%2FcMnoH)**

Thank you,

Tim Kaine | | [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=I8xJF0ltbdu%2B0wXs34qCkbdIWvw3tO%2B5)
---
| Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=hh0%2BoNtfeV3i91vRrv36pLdIWvw3tO%2B5).
---

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Remember this?
body:
#
Team --
When we first launched our campaign this past March, we knew we'd be the underdogs in the race. I didn't have a famous last name or the ability to write myself a big check. And as the _Tampa Bay Times_ noted yesterday: I'm the only non-millionaire Democrat running for Governor.
So I reached out to folks like you across the state, shared my story, and [**hoped you 'd pitch in whatever you could to help out.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3268/9e00690/4fc31f01/24031700/2352294687/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171024-nd")
Growing up, my grandmother always told us to go to school, mind our teachers, get our lesson, and bring that education home - for our brothers, our baby sister, and the kids in our neighborhood.
She wanted us to know we were responsible for more than ourselves -- we are also responsible for our family and our neighborhood, and everyone around us.
Like so many of you, I am here today because of the very policies Republicans say are "destroying" our great state of Florida. Our family at times relied on food assistance in Miami and Gainesville. My parents weren't college educated, but they both worked hard: my mother as a bus driver and my father as a construction worker.
I knew it wasn't enough for me to become the first in my immediate family to graduate high school and go to college -- it was my duty to make sure I wasn't the last. The Democratic Party and progressive philosophy created a way up and a way out for even families like mine, and so when I began my career in public service at just 23 years old, I did so as a progressive Democrat. My experiences reflected those of average, everyday Floridians. And I knew those people deserved a champion -- someone to bring it home. And that's why I'm running for Governor now, and why I'm reaching out to you again today.
Since we launched this campaign, thousands of dedicated supporters have pitched in whatever they could afford to make this people-powered movement possible. [**Can I count on you to join them today before our critical end of month deadline?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3268/9e00690/4fc31f01/24031700/2352294687/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171024-nd")
This is our opportunity to rebuild Florida into a state that works for all of us, not just the wealthy and powerful. I'm excited to see what we can accomplish together.
Bring it home,
Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3268/9e00690/4fc31f01/24031700/2352294687/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171024-nd")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3268/9e00690/4fc31f01/24031703/2352294687/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3268/9e00690/4fc31f01/24031702/2352294687/VEsD/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3268/9e00690/4fc31f01/24031705/2352294687/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

subject: "Rare opportunity" to win back Jeff Sessions' Senate seat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
NPR reported this weekend that we have "a rare opportunity" to win back the Alabama Senate seat in December's special election.
---
**[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171024-Battleground_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
_**NPR: "Democrats Eye A Rare Opportunity In Alabama Senate Race"**_
Team,

_NPR_ reported this weekend that we have "a rare opportunity" to win back the Alabama Senate seat in December's special election to fill Jeff Sessions' Senate seat.

Republican Roy Moore is quickly alienating Alabama voters with his extreme right-wing rhetoric and a recent _Fox News_ poll even showed the race tied.

We still face an uphill battle -- Alabama hasn't elected a Democratic Senator in more than 20 years -- but it's clear this state is competitive. And what happens next will depend on supporters like you.

[**Will you contribute $1 now to help fund our organizing efforts in Alabama and ensure our Democratic Senate candidates can compete in every state?**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171024-Battleground_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Doug Jones is the underdog in this race, but as he told _NPR_ this week, he's no stranger to taking on long shot fights.

As he said, "It's not the first time that I heard that something I wanted to do, something I was passionate about, was a long shot. When we prosecuted old Klansmen for the 1963 bombing of 16th Street Baptist Church, I was told, 'It's a long shot, Doug.'"

Doug overcame the odds then and now he's fighting his heart out to make sure Democrats compete for every vote in every state -- and he's counting on our support.

**Chip in $1 now to take on Republicans everywhere, starting with the Alabama special election:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/compete-in-every-state**](http://action.dscc. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Let's be clear about what Trump's doing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
President Trump's executive orders are a direct attack on the health and wellbeing of the American people.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MTIyMTIwNDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MjEzNDgyOQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, When it comes to President Trump's erratic and divisive actions, it's important to call them out for what they are. Case in point: His executive orders on health care that threaten the stability of the _Affordable Care Act_. They are nothing but an unconscionable attack on the health and financial security of millions of Americans -- including the middle class and the elderly -- and tens of thousands of folks in my state. [**That 's why I am fighting tooth and nail, calling on Congress to take action and reverse these executive orders -- add your name to my official petition to up the pressure. **](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MTIyMTIwNDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MjEzNDgyOQS2&mt=1&rt=0) The media might label his orders an attack on the _Affordable Care Act_ , or an attempt to go rogue and skirt the checks and balances of Congress -- but the consequences are more than that. It will mean more expensive health care and worse coverage for millions of people. **I 'll tell you this, as a governor of a state where nearly 2 million people now have health care thanks to President Obama's _Affordable Care Act_ , I'm seeing the consequences of President Trump's disastrous orders up close.** You and I know we can't count on the Trump administration to do the right thing. We know they're blinded by an angry partisan agenda, and won't work for the wellbeing of our people. So Congress must act -- leaders on both sides need to step up and stop Trump's sabotage of the American health care system by passing commonsense bipartisan solutions. [**Add your name right away to call on Congress to act: Reverse Trump 's disastrous executive orders and work together on behalf of the American people. **](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MTIyMTIwNDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MjEzNDgyOQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Honestly, it shouldn't take a petition. The president and Congress should be expected to act in good faith to better the lives of the American people. But we know we can't count on that -- not from this president, nor from Paul Ryan. So the American people, all of us, must step up. Our health and our financial security are at stake. So let's get to work. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MTIyMTIwNDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MjEzNDgyOQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Tom Perez
subject: Derek, Obamacare isn't about Barack at all
body:
Obamacare is here to stay.
---
**Republicans are doing everything they can to sabotage Obamacare. Pitch in $3 or more to help elect Democrats who will fight back.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523a6e/4067b0a/451ad10f6/7e64fc0c/2462514380/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171024_tp_nd&source=em_20171024_tp_nd&refcode=em_20171024_tp_nd")**

When I think about Obamacare, Derek, I think about my wife, Ann Marie, and our three kids. I think about the children all across this country and the parents caring for them. I think about the brave men and women who've shared their personal stories of how access to affordable care has changed their lives. I think about you.

But when our current president thinks about "Obamacare," he only sees another opportunity to undo President Obama's legacy.

He doesn't consider for a second the millions of families who count on the ACA for quality, affordable care. He doesn't care about women who, before the ACA, faced higher premiums simply because of their gender. He doesn't think about children who are born with a life-threatening illness and, without protections for pre-existing conditions, could be forced to pay astronomical rates for the rest of their lives.

But Democrats in Congress and all over the country are doing everything we can to save and protect Obamacare -- we're doing it for the millions of Americans who are counting on it.

If you're committed to helping elect Democrats who will protect our care, pitch in $3 or more now.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

body:
This Senate special election is tightening. [**Help us ensure Democrats have the best opportunity possible to win an upset in Alabama >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bab/70cdc2ba/46b79c6a/397d6eb/100440483/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-alspecial?refcode=em102417&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Help+us+ensure+Democrats+have+the+best+o&utm_campaign=em102417&source=em102417")
* * *
**TOP NEWS: Alabama Senate race tightening!**
**[Help Chris support Senate Democrats now>>](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bab/70cdc2ba/46b79c6a/397d6e8/100440483/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-alspecial?refcode=em102417&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Help+Chris+support+Senate+Democrats+now+&utm_campaign=em102417&source=em102417")**
After right-wing extremist Roy Moore's surprising win in Alabama's GOP Senate primary last month, all eyes turned to our Democratic nominee, Doug Jones.

Now, a new poll shows Jones and Moore are neck-and-neck. Democrats have a growing opportunity to defeat the alt-right's candidate, flip this seat, and help win back control of the Senate. But that will take a lot more work and a lot more support, and we have only 7 weeks left until the election.

[**Chris is working hard to support Doug Jones and Senate Democrats as we fight to win back the majority. Help us with a donation right now >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bab/70cdc2ba/46b79c6a/397d6e9/100440483/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-alspecial?refcode=em102417&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Chris+is+working+hard+to+support+Doug+Jo&utm_campaign=em102417&source=em102417")

As Chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC), Chris is focused on electing Democrats to the Senate across the nation. That means investing in critical and competitive races wherever they exist - even a special election in deep red Alabama.

The newest poll shows Democrat Doug Jones has a true chance to win in Alabama on December 12, but it won't be easy.

[**Help Chris make sure Doug Jones and Senate Democrats have the resources they need to fight and win >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bab/70cdc2ba/46b79c6a/397d6e6/100440483/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-alspecial?refcode=em102417&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=4+-+Help+Chris+make+sure+Doug+Jones+and+Sena&utm_campaign=em102417&source=em102417")

Thank you for helping on this next step to making 2018 a Democratic year in the U.S. Senate!

Van Hollen HQ

subject: re. tax reform

body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde49/2a380d31/24ad02f/fe1406/4197168022/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde49/2a380d31/24ad02f/fe1406/4197168022/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hello,
As a tax reform plan begins to take shape in Congress, we need to focus on one thing: helping hard-working families.
If we work together, we can find sensible solutions to grow our economy without adding to the deficit and putting a burden on our children and grandchildren.
Sound like a plan? Great. **[Put your name down to join our grassroots team urging Congress to pass a tax reform plan that would benefit hard-working families, not just corporate special interests and millionaires.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde49/2a380d31/24ad02f/fe1407/4197168022/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/protectworkingfamilies/?code=Hassan&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2017_taxreform-ja&20171024_taxreformja_up&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**
Unfortunately, Donald Trump and many Washington Republicans have different priorities.
Instead of making sure the single mother who works three jobs and the young person trying to get a start-up off the ground are taken care of, they want to offer tax breaks to the wealthiest among us -\- especially the Wall Street millionaires and hedge fund billionaires who backed their campaigns.
**Let 's make sure that Washington Republicans know that we expect them to work for the American people, not the wealthy few who buy their seats in Congress. [Please sign our petition to tell Congress that tax reform should help hard-working Americans.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde49/2a380d31/24ad02f/fe1407/4197168022/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/protectworkingfamilies/?code=Hassan&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2017_taxreform-ja&20171024_taxreformja_up&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**
Middle class families have enough to worry about without an increase to their tax burden, so thank you for taking action.
-- Team Maggie
